thread.title: Body cam footage becomes focus in trial against man charged with killing Cobb County teen
thread.title_full: Body cam footage becomes focus in trial against man charged with killing Cobb County teen

author: Adam Murphy
title: Body cam footage becomes focus in trial against man charged with killing Cobb County teen
text:
ATLANTA, Ga. (Atlanta News First) - Police bodycam footage took center stage this week inside Cobb County Superior Court in the trial against 68-year-old Jerome Cox.
Cox is charged with vehicular homicide in the first degree after allegedly hitting 17-year-old Olivia Pugh last December on Ben King Road in Kennesaw.
“He stated probably three beers,” Former Cobb County Police Officer Steven Oney said.
According to Cobb County Police, Cox was driving under the influence of alcohol at the time of the incident. Police said Pugh was with a group of other teens crossing the road at a marked crosswalk when the car hit Pugh, sending her flying into a wooded area on the side of the road. She died later. Former officer Steven Oney responded to the scene that night and was questioned on the stand.
“There were actually three separate occasions that I spoke with him. The first two occasions his wife, Jaime Cox, was standing next to him and I could smell the odor of alcoholic beverage around them,” Oney said.
The defense argued that their client was clearly capable of driving and cooperated with investigators on scene.
The defense also tried to poke holes in the credibility of the investigation arguing that the sergeant presiding over the incident turned his bodycam off just 15-minutes after arriving on scene. When asked if he was on scene longer than 15-minutes this is how he responded.
“Just a little bit,” Cobb County Police Sgt. Nicholas Leone said.
